svg.text: Every module interconnected by default — and deployable standalone when needed. No flagships: flagship is a status, not an architecture.
grounded in maple-suite · maple-quotations · Maple-Photoshoot · MapleLens
EDGE — Caddy 2 (auto-TLS · reverse proxy · one block per subdomain)

g.text: Host header → getBrand() → Tenant row → logo · colors · company
web = public site · admin = tenant settings · users = user mgmt

svg.text: IDENTITY — one login for all 19 apps

g.text: jose JWT in mt_session cookie · cookie domain = SSO_DOMAIN
shared AUTH_SECRET verifies everywhere — no token exchange
RBAC: Role model + permissions.ts per module action
flags.ts (Flipt server): tools on/off per plan → ToolDisabled
standalone mode reuses the same JWT claim shape (see frame 6)
C1 shared DB records — one Client/Product/Tenant/User + cross-module FKs (Order→Quotation,
Payment→Invoice) · modules join in SQL, not over HTTP
C2 single session — one JWT cookie on the parent domain; every app verifies with the same secret
C3 async events — OutboxEvent same-TX write pattern (quotation.accepted → orders) · model exists
in maple-quotations, no writer yet — build writers + dispatcher on fold-in
C4 HTTP APIs — each app's app/api/* REST routes · own UI now, detached modules & integrations
C5 files & assets — storage.ts / filestream.ts · bytes in Postgres today → S3-compatible at fleet scale

svg.text: 3 · MODULE APPS — apps/* · one Next.js app per module (Turborepo)
Each chip = its own container on its own subdomain. ANY module can be promoted to customer-grade — promotion = harden + flag on, never a rewrite.

g.text: app/ UI routes (App Router) in SuiteShell
app/api/* REST handlers — the module's contract
same Docker image · own container · port 3000
no module imports another module's code —
they meet only via C1–C5 channels (frame 2)
→ that boundary is what makes standalone possible
identity auth.ts session.ts sso.ts (jose) · rbac.ts permissions.ts
ui SuiteShell · PageHeader · ToolDisabled · ui/* kit · theme.css
domain helpers: hr.ts clientLink.ts nav.ts slug.ts utils.ts (tested)
prisma/schema.prisma — 22 models, one source of truth · Prisma 6 singleton
standalone repos vendor both packages via tsconfig paths

svg.text: 5 · DATA LAYER — one Postgres per deployment, tenant-scoped everywhere
CORE / SHARED TABLES — every module reads these
MODULE-OWNED TABLES — written by exactly one module

g.text: Lead · Quotation · Order · Invoice · Payment · DeliveryChallan

svg.text: THE MONEY CHAIN (cross-module foreign keys — channel C1)
tenancy: every business row carries tenantId — tenantDb() Prisma extension auto-filters reads + stamps writes (incl. upsert) · one DB serves many tenants, or one tenant per instance
6 · STANDALONE MODE — run any module alone (proven pattern)

g.text: ① vendor @maple/core + db via tsconfig paths — no npm publish
② own schema: core subset (Tenant·User·Client) + module models
③ own Postgres · own jose-JWT login · own domain
④ contract back to suite: C3 events + C4 REST · same JWT claims
→ folds back behind its module flag;
repo stays as the standalone template

svg.text: 7 · AI & DOCUMENT SERVICES — shared plumbing, module-agnostic

g.text: pdf-to-img + sharp: page render → crop → thumbnails
keys+model per tenant: AppSetting → env → default
all branded from the Tenant row (logo·banner·GSTIN)
open item: bundle a font for the ₹ glyph
fine to a few dozen customers
built — a switch, not a rewrite

svg.text: CI/CD: GitHub Actions → docker build → registry → compose pull/up · gates: Vitest (unit) + Playwright (e2e)

g.text: separate Next.js app (repo: MapleLens) · Supabase · consumer funnel — NOT a suite module, shares the AI approach, not code
future: MapleID bridge — Lens signups land in the suite as Leads (via C3 event or C4 REST)
photoshoot's generation wizard already carries the Lens template/slot engine inside the suite
① one stack ruling: core jose-JWT + Postgres vs Supabase (blocks photoshoot fold-in)
② asset bytes: Postgres → S3-compatible object storage ③ OutboxEvent dispatcher: poller vs webhooks
④ MapleID token shared across suite + MapleLens ⑤ bundle ₹-capable font for PDFs

svg.text: Every box maps to code that exists today — repos: maple-suite (apps/* + packages/*), maple-quotations, Maple-Photoshoot, MapleLens. Read top-down: edge → apps → packages → data; frames 2 and 6 are the contract that keeps modules independent.
